original_hypothesis.title: ILOPool.buy uses ERC721Enumerable function without inheriting it, potentially bricking buys or enabling inconsistent tokenId selection
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: ILOPool.buy() calls tokenOfOwnerByIndex(recipient, 0) to retrieve an existing position tokenId, but ILOPool inherits OZ ERC721 (not ERC721Enumerable) in the provided code. If ERC721Enumerable is not actually present in the compiled inheritance tree, buy() cannot compile or will revert/behave unexpectedly. Even if a custom ERC721 implementation is used elsewhere, relying on enumeration for a 'single position per user' rule can be fragile under transfers/burns, causing tokenId selection mismatches and incorrect position updates.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: This is a concrete code defect signal: a function call to an extension that is not visible in inheritance. If true, the main sale entrypoint fails entirely or exhibits inconsistent tokenId resolution (breaking accounting invariants like totalRaised consistency).
deployment_assumptions: The deployed ILOPool bytecode matches the shown source (inherits OZ ERC721 without ERC721Enumerable)
No custom ERC721 implementation providing tokenOfOwnerByIndex is used at compile time
original_hypothesis.config_evidence: No deployment artifact/flattened source proving ERC721Enumerable inclusion was provided; code shows `import '@openzeppelin/contracts/token/ERC721/ERC721.sol';` only.

proof_of_signal.pattern: buy() calls tokenOfOwnerByIndex(recipient, 0) but contract inherits ERC721 (no enumerable extension shown)

preconditions: Recipient already owns an ILOPool NFT so the else-branch executes
tokenOfOwnerByIndex is not implemented (or is implemented inconsistently with expected semantics)

initial_code_evidence.specific_concern: buy() may be non-functional or may select wrong tokenId for position updates
initial_code_evidence.missing_safeguards: No alternative mapping from owner=>tokenId; no explicit enumeration inheritance in provided code

analysis_questions: Does ILOPool actually inherit ERC721Enumerable or an equivalent implementation in the real compilation unit?
If enumerable is present, can tokenOfOwnerByIndex(recipient,0) ever return an unexpected tokenId after transfers/burns causing position/accounting corruption?
Is the intended invariant 'one token per recipient' enforced elsewhere (e.g., transfer hooks) or purely by balanceOf==0 check?
validation_requirements: Confirm compiled inheritance tree and ABI contains tokenOfOwnerByIndex
If present, fuzz transfers + multiple buys to ensure tokenId selection always targets the intended single position
Add unit test ensuring buy() works after NFT transfers, burns (refund), and re-minting

original_hypothesis.preliminary_attack_scenario: Attacker (or normal user) with an existing position triggers buy() else-branch; if tokenOfOwnerByIndex is missing or broken, buy() reverts, halting further contributions and potentially preventing reaching softCap. If it returns an unintended tokenId, the contract may credit liquidity/raiseAmount to the wrong position, desyncing totalRaised vs sum(raiseAmount).
prerequisites: At least one position NFT exists for a recipient

potential_impact_if_confirmed.financial_impact: Sale can be permanently or intermittently bricked; or accounting may corrupt leading to incorrect refunds/claims
potential_impact_if_confirmed.affected_users: All buyers after first mint to a recipient; potentially all participants if buys halt

original_hypothesis.test_coverage_gap: No evidence of tests covering second buy() for same recipient and enumeration behavior

technical_investigation_focus.detection_analysis: ABI/inheritance verification + runtime tests on deployed bytecode

validation_result.mechanism: False positive: OZ v3.x ERC721 includes enumerable
validation_result.impact_summary: No defect. OpenZeppelin v3.x ERC721 (used with Solidity 0.7.6) includes tokenOfOwnerByIndex in the base contract, unlike v4.x where it was separated.
validation_result.detailed_analysis: The hypothesis claims that ILOPool.buy() calls tokenOfOwnerByIndex() which is an ERC721Enumerable function not available in the base ERC721. However, this is incorrect for the OpenZeppelin version used. The project compiles with Solidity 0.7.6 (foundry.toml: solc-version = "0.7.6"), which means it uses OpenZeppelin v3.x contracts (the only version compatible with Solidity 0.7.6). In OpenZeppelin v3.x, the base ERC721.sol contract includes ALL enumerable functionality (tokenOfOwnerByIndex, totalSupply, tokenByIndex) as part of the base implementation. The separation of ERC721 and ERC721Enumerable into distinct contracts only occurred in OpenZeppelin v4.x (which requires Solidity ^0.8.0). Therefore, the import `@openzeppelin/contracts/token/ERC721/ERC721.sol` in the v3.x library DOES provide tokenOfOwnerByIndex, and the buy() function will compile and execute correctly.
validation_result.code_evidence_assessment: ILOPool.sol line 9 imports '@openzeppelin/contracts/token/ERC721/ERC721.sol'. The remappings.txt maps '@openzeppelin/=lib/openzeppelin-contracts'. Since the project uses Solidity 0.7.6 (foundry.toml), the OpenZeppelin library must be v3.x. In OZ v3.x, ERC721.sol includes _tokenOfOwnerByIndex functionality via internal mappings (_holderTokens using EnumerableMap and _tokenOwners using EnumerableSet). The function tokenOfOwnerByIndex(address,uint256) is defined in the base ERC721 contract in v3.x. The Phase A architecture analysis also confirms this: the token semantics section notes 'ERC721 with enumerable' for ILOPool NFTs.

calling_sequence: 2. If balanceOf > 0, calls tokenOfOwnerByIndex(recipient, 0) at line 113
3. tokenOfOwnerByIndex is provided by OZ v3.x base ERC721 contract
4. Function correctly returns the first token ID owned by recipient
execution_path_analysis.vulnerable_code_path: The code path is valid. tokenOfOwnerByIndex(recipient, 0) at ILOPool.sol:113 is a function inherited from OpenZeppelin v3.x ERC721, which includes enumerable functionality in the base contract. The function will correctly return the token ID at index 0 for the given owner. Since ILOPool enforces balanceOf(recipient) != 0 before reaching this branch, and the single-position-per-user invariant is maintained by this if/else logic (new mint only when balance is 0), tokenOfOwnerByIndex(recipient, 0) will always return the user's single position token ID.

deployment_assumptions_checked: The deployed ILOPool bytecode matches the shown source (inherits OZ ERC721 without ERC721Enumerable)
No custom ERC721 implementation providing tokenOfOwnerByIndex is used at compile time
config_validation.deployment_evidence: foundry.toml specifies solc-version = "0.7.6". remappings.txt maps @openzeppelin/ to lib/openzeppelin-contracts. For Solidity 0.7.6 compatibility, this must be OpenZeppelin v3.x. In OZ v3.x, the base ERC721.sol contract includes full enumerable functionality (tokenOfOwnerByIndex, totalSupply, tokenByIndex) as part of the base implementation. Deploy.s.sol creates ILOPool with `new ILOPool{salt: salt}()` confirming standard compilation.

validation_result.attack_scenario_validation: The attack scenario is invalid. The hypothesis assumes tokenOfOwnerByIndex is missing or broken, but it is correctly provided by the OpenZeppelin v3.x ERC721 base contract. The buy() function works correctly: when a recipient already has a position (balanceOf > 0), tokenOfOwnerByIndex(recipient, 0) returns their existing token ID, and the position is updated accordingly. The function will not revert, will not return an unexpected token ID (assuming no transfers, which is the common case), and the sale will proceed normally.
validation_result.impact_assessment: No impact. The code compiles and functions correctly because OpenZeppelin v3.x ERC721 includes enumerable functionality. Note: there IS a separate real concern about NFT transfers breaking the single-position-per-user invariant (if user transfers NFT away, balanceOf becomes 0, allowing a new mint), but that is a different issue (per-user cap bypass via INV-ACCESS-001) and is not what this hypothesis is about.
validation_result.replication_plan: 1. Verify OZ version in lib/openzeppelin-contracts is v3.x by checking package.json or tag. 2. Confirm ERC721.sol in that version contains tokenOfOwnerByIndex. 3. Compile ILOPool.sol with solc 0.7.6 and verify no compilation errors. 4. Write test: deploy ILOPool clone, initialize, buy once for a recipient, buy again for same recipient, verify second buy uses existing token ID via tokenOfOwnerByIndex.
uncertainties: The exact OpenZeppelin version in lib/openzeppelin-contracts is not directly visible, but Solidity 0.7.6 compatibility constrains it to v3.x which includes enumerable in base ERC721
additional_testing_needed: Verify the exact OpenZeppelin contracts version in the lib directory
Test NFT transfer scenarios where tokenOfOwnerByIndex might return unexpected results if user has transferred previous NFTs (separate issue from this hypothesis)
